root_text.an3.43:1.1: “Tayo, bhikkhave, atthavase sampassamānena alameva paresaṁ dhammaṁ desetuṁ.
root_text.an3.43:1.3: Yo dhammaṁ deseti so atthappaṭisaṁvedī ca hoti dhammappaṭisaṁvedī ca.
root_text.an3.43:1.4: Yo dhammaṁ suṇāti so atthappaṭisaṁvedī ca hoti dhammappaṭisaṁvedī ca.
root_text.an3.43:1.5: Yo ceva dhammaṁ deseti yo ca dhammaṁ suṇāti ubho atthappaṭisaṁvedino ca honti dhammappaṭisaṁvedino ca.
root_text.an3.43:1.6: Ime kho, bhikkhave, tayo atthavase sampassamānena alameva paresaṁ dhammaṁ desetun”ti.

translation_text.an3.43:1.1: “Mendicants, taking three reasons into consideration provides quite enough motivation to teach Dhamma to another.
translation_text.an3.43:1.3: When the teacher understands the meaning and the teaching.
translation_text.an3.43:1.4: When the audience understands the meaning and the teaching.
translation_text.an3.43:1.5: When both the teacher and the audience understand the meaning and the teaching.
translation_text.an3.43:1.6: Taking these three reasons into consideration provides quite enough motivation to teach Dhamma to another.”
